data.title: Snowflake Q3 bookings up 50%, but stock falls; analysts maintain ratings with increased price targets. RPO at $7.9B, bookings at nearly $1B, AI ARR $100M. Product revenue growth slightly lower than expected.
data.description: Snowflake Inc.'s (NYSE: SNOW) shares tumbled despite positive third-quarter results. Analysts' ratings and price targets for SNOW were revealed: Cantor Fitzgerald raised target to $278; Scotiabank to $290; Canaccord Genuity to $270; KeyBanc Capital Markets to $285; JPMorgan to $268; Goldman Sachs to $275; Wedbush to $270; DA Davidson to $300; BTIG to $312; Needham to $280; Guggenheim Securities maintained Neutral; BofA Securities to $310. Cantor Fitzgerald praised Snowflake's Q3 results with $7.9b in RPO, 37.5% growth rate, and $1b in net new RPO. Scotiabank noted a slight deceleration in product revenue growth and a performance lower than last quarter. Additional details on Benzinga.com.

data.title: C3.ai's Q2 revenue of $75.1 million beat analysts' expectations, impressing investors and leading to stock rise.
data.description: C3.ai Inc (NYSE: AI) saw a rise in shares during early trading following its positive fiscal second-quarter earnings report. Analyst Dan Ives from Wedbush reiterated an Outperform rating with a price target of $20. Canaccord Genuity's Kingsley Crane maintained a Hold rating with a $16 price target. DA Davidson's Lucky Schreiner reaffirmed an Underperform rating and $13 price target. Analyst Mike Cikos from Needham kept a Hold rating on the stock. C3.ai reported total revenue of $75.1 million for the quarter, which slightly missed its guidance range but exceeded the $74.9 million consensus. The company, under new CEO Stephen Ehikian, aims to recover from previous setbacks. Despite challenges like the government shutdown, C3.ai's federal business showed positive momentum in the third quarter. Full details are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: YC F25 startup Multifactor raises $15M seed round led by Nexus Venture Partners, ensuring secure online account access for humans and AI agents.
data.description: In a recent funding round, Nexus Venture Partners took the lead as demand significantly increased for a secure and shareable way to access online accounts. The San Francisco-based company Multifactor, known for their post-quantum security platform, is paving the way for the safest online account access for humans and AI agents. This surge in demand is a strong indication of the growing need for provably secure online interactions. The information was disclosed on December 4, 2025.
OneDigital completes strategic investment by Stone Point Capital and CPP Investments, announced by Onex Partners.

data.title: Bullfrog Power unveils Algorand (ALGO) sustainability certificates for Staples Canada, boosting transparency in eco initiatives.
data.description: Bullfrog Power has recently launched tokenized environmental certificates on the Algorand (ALGO) blockchain to bolster transparency in sustainability measures. Staples Canada has signed on as the inaugural participant in this initiative. This move aims to increase accountability and traceability in environmental efforts. The introduction of these certificates demonstrates a commitment to sustainability practices and provides a cutting-edge approach to monitoring environmental impact. This collaboration signifies a step forward in promoting eco-friendly operations within the business sector.

data.title: pH7 Technologies raises $25.6M in Series B led by Fine Structure Ventures for critical metals extraction technology.
data.description: pH7 Technologies Inc., based in Vancouver, Canada, a company specializing in the extraction of critical metals at a commercial scale, has successfully completed the initial phase of its Series B funding round. The company raised a significant amount of USD $25.6 million in this round. The funding was primarily led by Fine Structure Ventures, a key role played by BHP Ventures as a strategic investor. Other notable investors that joined in this round include Energy & Environment Investment (EEI), Siteground, Gaingels Fund, and Calm Venture. Additionally, existing investors also contributed to the funding.

data.title: Private sector activity in B.C. weak, with GDP growth below 2% in 2026-2027. Completion of $100 billion construction projects a factor.
data.description: The Business Council of British Columbia released a report titled "Searching for the Private Sector's Lost Mojo" on the economic conditions in B.C. and Canada. The report indicates a challenging outlook due to global economic weaknesses. B.C. is experiencing slow growth with projected GDP growth below 2% in 2026 and 2027, lower than the long-term average of 2.5%. This slight decrease could result in billions of dollars of lost economic activity, affecting jobs, incomes, and public services. The completion of major construction projects totaling $100 billion has contributed to the economic slowdown, as the private sector lacks activity to replace them. B.C. faces the largest operating deficit in Canada relative to its economy. The full article is available on Benzinga.com.
THE TRUMP ADMINISTRATION WILL SHOWCASE SOUTH KOREA AND ISRAEL AS "MODEL ALLIES" IN THE NEW NATIONAL DEFENSE STRATEGY, ACCORDING TO NIKKEI.

data.title: Beer Institute reports 11,000,000 barrels of taxable removals in October 2025, down 3.8% from 11,434,193 in 2024.
data.description: WASHINGTON, Dec. 04, 2025 -- It has been reported that the unofficial estimate of taxable removals for October 2025 stands at 11,000,000 barrels, showing a 3.8% decrease compared to the October 2024 removals, which were reported at 11,434,193. The Taxable Removals Tax Paid by TTB for both years in terms of 31 Gallon Barrels showed a 8.6% decrease from 11,571,405 in January 2024 to 10,572,389 in January 2025 amounting to a volume change of -999,016 barrels. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Sean McLaren elected as American Wood Council Board Chair for a two-year term, along with new board members.
data.description: Leesburg, Virginia - On December 4, 2025, the American Wood Council (AWC) disclosed the appointment of Sean McLaren, President and CEO of West Fraser, as the new Board Chair for a two-year period. McLaren will lead the board alongside Derek, the CEO of SmartLam. Additionally, new board members were named and a Second Vice Chair was appointed. The board changes aim to bring fresh perspectives to the AWC leadership team.

data.title: Chainalysis improves pricing support by integrating with CoinGecko, offering clients updated digital asset data for better analytics and security.
data.description: CoinGecko has recently partnered with Chainalysis to strengthen pricing support for customers. This collaboration aims to offer users detailed and current digital asset information to enhance analytics and security measures. This strategic partnership will provide valuable insights and data on a wide range of cryptocurrencies, empowering users with vital information for their investment strategies.

data.title: Fabrix.ai presents AI platform at summit, concludes first-of-its-kind virtual event. Will present at Gartner IOCS Conference.
data.description: In a groundbreaking event, Fabrix.ai recently wrapped up a unique virtual summit in collaboration with SiliconAngle and ZK Research. The summit, titled "Agentic AI Unleashed: The Future of Digital & IT," took place in Pleasanton, Calif. The company is set to showcase its innovative strategies at the upcoming Gartner IOCS Conference from December 9 to 11. Stay tuned for more updates on this exciting development.

data.title: AIIR Products partners with Nelson & Co to expand AI-driven HVAC solutions in Florida, targeting hospitality, student housing with sustainable climate solutions.
data.description: Partnership formed to implement advanced, eco-friendly climate solutions in the hospitality, student housing, and multi-family housing sectors across North and Central Florida. Based in Austin, Texas, AIIR Products (AIIR) is spearheading this initiative as a prominent supplier of AI-driven heating and cooling systems. This collaboration aims to introduce intelligent and sustainable technologies to a wide range of properties. The agreement, established on December 4, 2025, demonstrates a commitment to enhancing environmental efficiency in the region.

data.title: FaverGray completed Aperture, a 680-bed student housing near UCF with 204 units, in Orlando.
data.description: In Orlando, Florida, FaverGray has recently completed the construction of Aperture, a student housing community called Aperture near the University of Central Florida (UCF), boasting 204 units with a total of 680 beds. This project was successfully finalized in Fall 2025 for Toll Brothers.

data.title: SweatHouz franchise expands across US, opening 2 new locations weekly until 2025, meeting demand for tech-based recovery suites.
data.description: With a surge in demand for tech-driven recovery suites, the contrast therapy franchise, SweatHouz (SWTHZ), is rapidly expanding into key markets across the United States. This growth comes as territories are quickly filling up, showcasing the increasing popularity of private recovery facilities. This expansion highlights the company's move into major U.S. cities, with Atlanta as a key focus. The franchise's push into these markets aims to capitalize on the growing trend, with a sharp increase in the number of individuals seeking out innovative contrast-therapy solutions for their recovery needs.

data.title: Newmark Group, Inc. appoints Peter Trollope to lead Occupier Solutions, enhancing corporate client advisory. Nasdaq: NMRK.
data.description: Improving Strategic Advisory Services for Corporate Customers NYC, Dec. 4, 2025 - Newmark Group, Inc. (Nasdaq: NMRK), a prominent commercial real estate consultancy serving major institutional investors and global corporations, is enhancing its strategic advisory capabilities.
